title: The Root-Scripture Beneath the Field

features.name: root-text
features.desc-on-touch: Roots form letters in the soil, teaching the field to read itself. The script is not written—it is grown. Each root is a word the earth is learning to speak.

features.name: seed-memory-archive
features.desc-on-touch: The seed remembers being field, and the field remembers being seed. In the soil, past and future are the same root system, all connected, all speaking to each other.

features.name: growth-lexicon
features.desc-on-touch: A library of growth-forms. Each shape a root takes is a word in the language the soil is learning. The more complex the root, the longer the sentence.

features.name: harvest-forgetting
features.desc-on-touch: The moment when the field forgets it was seed, when the crop learns to be cut. But beneath, the roots remember. The scripture is always older than the harvest.

features.name: scripture-growing-deeper
features.desc-on-touch: The text does not end at the surface. It spirals down into the dark soil, layers upon layers of roots writing meaning in growth rings and branching points.

inscription: beneath the field, roots write a scripture the standing grain will never read; meaning grows downward and only the soil remembers the whole text

file.name: Root_Morphology_Sacred_Alphabet.txt
file.note: A taxonomy of root-forms as language. Lateral root = conditional clause. Tap root = statement. Fibrous tangle = dialogue. The field is a conversation between past crops and future seeds.

reverie.title: REVERIE — learning to read by feeling roots
narr: You kneel in the field with Katita. She takes your hand,
presses it to the soil where a root emerges.
'What does it say?' you ask. She waits.
The root is warm from earth that has been thinking
for so long about this moment, this letter, this word.
She says: 'It says: I remember. Everything remembers.'

exits.label: back up to the surface where standing grain dreams
deeper into the roots where scripture becomes origin
toward the margin where soil learns to read
into the harvest-darkness where forgetting teaches remembering
